mechanism_narrative: A lower level of the federal funds rate reduces the risk-free discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows, mechanically increasing present equity values and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward returns is positive or statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor income and household demand, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 50% of months where UNRATE rises by more than 0.2 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Joint month-over-month increases in the federal funds rate and the unemployment rate indicate restrictive monetary policy hitting a weakening labor market, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases month-over-month AND UNRATE increases month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 60-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances where both FEDFUNDS and UNRATE increase simultaneously.

mechanism_narrative: Strong industrial production growth during a monetary tightening cycle signals an overheating supply side that invites further Fed rate increases, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Positive 60-day returns occur in more than 50% of triggered instances during periods when FEDFUNDS is in an uptrend.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month expansion in industrial production reflects rising corporate output and earnings capacity, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO and 5-day forward returns is negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.05) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1500 thousand units signals a contraction in residential investment and downstream demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of observations when HOUST is below 1500 thousand units.

mechanism_narrative: Rising payroll employment concurrent with falling retail sales indicates labor hoarding into a revenue downturn, squeezing corporate margins and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS increases month-over-month AND RSAFS decreases month-over-month
failure_condition: Negative 20-day returns occur in fewer than 45% of instances where PAYEMS rises while RSAFS falls.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate exceeding 5% restricts credit availability and raises corporate borrowing costs enough to depress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of months when FEDFUNDS exceeds 5%.
